etymology.description: semantic 糹(糹) (mì, "silk; thread") + phonetic 及(及) (jí) -> thread sequence -> level; grade
etymology.interpretation: The character 級 (jí, "level; grade") combines 糹(糹) (mì, "silk; thread") for the idea of a sequence, such as threads being arranged, with 及(及) (jí) providing the sound. The association with a sequence of threads leads to the concept of progression or arrangement, hence the meaning of level or grade.
etymology.simplification: 級 (jí, "level; grade") was simplified by replacing 糹(糹) (mì, "silk; thread") with 纟 for ease of writing while retaining the other component 及(及) (jí).

examples.chinese: 学生们的成绩很好，达到了及格的级别(學生們的成績很好，達到了及格的級別)。
examples.pinyin: Xuéshēngmen de chéngjī hěn hǎo, dádào le jígé de jíbié.
examples.english: The students' grades are good, reaching a passing level.

examples.chinese: 这条河流的水位已经超过了警戒级别(這條河流的水位已經超過了警戒級別)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè tiáo héliú de shuǐwèi yǐjīng chāoguòle jǐngjiè jíbié.
examples.english: The water level of this river has already exceeded the warning level.

examples.chinese: 这个考试分为多个难度级(這個考試分為多個難度級)，学生可以根据自己的水平选择(學生可以根據自己的水平選擇)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège kǎoshì fēn wéi duō gè nándù jí, xuéshēng kěyǐ gēnjù zìjǐ de shuǐpíng xuǎnzé.
examples.english: This test is divided into multiple difficulty levels, and students can choose based on their own level.
